description_long: This is a sleek, twin-fuselage airplane with a streamlined, modern design. The aircraft features two parallel fuselages connected by a single, straight wing and a horizontal stabilizer at the rear. Each fuselage is equipped with a propeller engine mounted at the nose, and the cockpit is located in the left fuselage, identifiable by its windows. The airplane has a conventional tail design with twin vertical stabilizers at the ends of the horizontal stabilizer. The landing gear is not visible, suggesting it may be retractable. The exterior is primarily a light gray or white color with subtle dark accents on the wingtips and stabilizers.
description: A modern, twin-fuselage airplane with dual nose-mounted propellers, a straight wing, and twin vertical stabilizers. The cockpit is located in the left fuselage, and the exterior is light gray with dark accents.
description_view_0: Side view showing both fuselages, the cockpit windows on the left fuselage, and the twin propellers at the front.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the twin vertical stabilizers and the horizontal stabilizer connecting both fuselages.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the right fuselage without cockpit windows and the overall symmetry of the design.
description_view_3: Front view showing the dual propellers, the parallel fuselages, and the straight wing configuration.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight configuration connecting twin fuselages, no visible winglets, slight dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Twin nose-mounted piston propeller engines, one on each fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers at the ends of a horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable based on design.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit in the left fuselage with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
